title: nymphaea alba var. chlorocarpa
intro: Nymphaea alba var. chlorocarpa, also known as the white water lily, is a submerged aquatic plant native to Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, it has been reported to possess medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Nymphaea alba var. chlorocarpa include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interaction with other medications.
